_doc: Byte-to-Unicode map for the OLCKOptimum-Medium and OLCKOptimum-ExtraBlack legacy display fonts. Their cmaps address Ol Chiki glyph outlines with printable ASCII source codes, so extracted PDF text requires a legacy-byte conversion. The public source is the Internet Archive item aale-chhatka-pdf-e-magazine-2023, PDF SHA-256 7588dae38adb5533e5692bf6cf6148cb5e411afa32c4f24dfe7cd1db1b9ec9b8; further evidence is documented in docs/EVIDENCE.md.
_derivation: Derived from normalized rendered-outline comparison against Unicode Ol Chiki references, constrained by corpus position and the structure of the Ol Chiki alphabet. The Optimum Medium and ExtraBlack fonts share this effective layout. OLCKLatic is a separately evidenced layout applied through the fixed overlay in olchiki.py; it is not encoded in this Optimum base resource.
_confidence: The confirmed inventory contains 52 letter or modifier sources, ten digit sources, and one punctuation source; no uncertain Optimum source remains. Lowercase n maps to U+1C71 OL CHIKI LETTER EN and uppercase T maps to U+1C5B OL CHIKI LETTER AT. Twenty uppercase and lowercase source pairs have identical outlines and intentionally share targets; d/D, h/H, m/M, n/N, o/O, and t/T are distinct. Uppercase W and X are included from exact outline identity with lowercase w and x rather than corpus occurrence.
